verses.1: In the LORD put I my trust: how say ye to my soul, Flee [as] a bird to your mountain?
verses.2: For, lo, the wicked bend [their] bow, they make ready their arrow upon the string, that they may privily shoot at the upright in heart.
verses.3: If the foundations be destroyed, what can the righteous do?
verses.4: The LORD [is] in his holy temple, the LORD'S throne [is] in heaven: his eyes behold, his eyelids try, the children of men.
verses.5: The LORD trieth the righteous: but the wicked and him that loveth violence his soul hateth.
verses.6: Upon the wicked he shall rain snares, fire and brimstone, and an horrible tempest: [this shall be] the portion of their cup.
verses.7: For the righteous LORD loveth righteousness; his countenance doth behold the upright.
